case_title: STEPHEN KRIVY VS. JANET M BOESSENECKER et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT JANET BOESSENECKER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. NO OPPOSITION FILED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

CASE DISMISSED AS TO ENTIRE ACTION BY COURT AT ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING MAR-26-2007 AT 1:30PM. THE COURT FINDS NO GOOD CAUSE OR SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDERS AND RULES. A LESS SEVERE SANCTION WOULD NOT BE EFFECTIVE DUE TO THE HISTORY OF LACK OF COMPLIANCE. PROCEEDINGS REPORTED BY: PATTY VAN STEENBERGE CSR# 1742. (212)

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-26-2007 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. The court having reviewed the file finds no good cause or substantial justification for plaintiff's failure to comply with the court's orders and rules. Therefore, it is hereby ordered that this matter is dismissed. The court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. Proceedings reported by: Patty Van Steenberge CSR# 1742. (212)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-26-2006 Continued To Mar-26-07 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Plaintiff in Propria Persona is sanctioned $350.00, for failure to appear. The OSC is re-set to 3/26/2007 at 1:30 PM, in Department 212. Proceedings reported by: Floyd L. Butler, CSR#2518 (212)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-01-2006 Set For Order To Show Cause On Dec-26-06 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-21-2006 Continued To Nov-09-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-10-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Janet Boessenecker, 392-3374, defendant and moving party appearing in propria persona

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JANET BOESSENECKER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Order signed in open Court. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189
